journal_entry.description: Customer deposit received for moving services to be performed in one month
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. The $5,000 deposit is received before services are performed, creating a liability (unearned revenue/deferred revenue). The cash asset increases by $5,000 (debit), and a liability for unearned revenue increases by $5,000 (credit). The tuples correctly indicate one asset increase (cash) and one liability increase (unearned revenue). No tax is applicable on this deposit as it represents an advance payment; HST/GST will be recognized when the service is actually performed and revenue is earned.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas debits its bank/chequing account (asset increase). The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue) on the credit side since the moving services have not yet been performed. Only 1 debit line: the cash/bank asset increase.

debit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas debits its bank/chequing account (asset increase). The deposit creates a liability (unearned revenue) on the credit side since the moving services have not yet been performed. Only 1 debit line: the cash/bank asset increase.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, which is NOT a credit-side event (assets are debited when increased). Actually, the deposit creates a liability because Atlas owes the customer moving services in the future. The $5,000 received creates an unearned revenue/customer deposit liability = liability increase (1 line). No revenue is recognized yet because the services will be performed in one month, not at signing.

credit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, which is NOT a credit-side event (assets are debited when increased). Actually, the deposit creates a liability because Atlas owes the customer moving services in the future. The $5,000 received creates an unearned revenue/customer deposit liability = liability increase (1 line). No revenue is recognized yet because the services will be performed in one month, not at signing.
